free_text: Excitation of low-lying levels and giant multipole
resonances in 92Zr, 120Sn and 208Pb by inelastic
scattering of 104 MeV polarized protons.
Atomic beam polarized ion source.
Beam polarization 65-70%.
Beam polarization determined using 4He(p,inl)
polarimeter.
Collective Model calculations.
No reply from S. Kailas to author proof.
VM
Data sets T0152010-015 converted from data set O0683
compiled by CAJaD and updated.
Zr Targets enriched to 95% 92Zr, thicknesses ranging
from approx. 1-25 mg/cm**2, located in the center of a
163-cm scattering chamber.
QDDM spectrograph.
Statistical uncertainty.
Tabulated data received from S. Kailas, 25 April 2001.

Zr Targets enriched to 95% 92Zr, thicknesses ranging
from approx. 1-25 mg/cm**2, located in the center of a
163-cm scattering chamber.
Delta(E)-E telescope consisting of intrinsic
germanium detectors of thickness 1 and 1.5-cm.
Overall energy resolution of better than 250-keV (FWHM)
. Beam current on the target varied from approx.
5 to approx. 150 nA.
Corrected for reaction losses in Ge detectors.
Statistical uncertainty scanned from figure.
Scanning uncertainty in data 7%; in angle 0.3 degrees.
Standard deviations are calculated by
averaging the excitation energy obtained for the
various angles in the fitting process.
Scanned by CAJAD from Fig.12 and 13 in
reference.
